minds.title: Motor Assembly Reliability
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: The motor assembly shall operate reliably for a minimum of 20,000 hours under rated load without degradation beyond acceptable limits.

type_specific_attributes.content: Motor assembly shall achieve MTBF >= 20,000 hours at rated load and ambient temperature range -20C to +55C.
type_specific_attributes.acceptance_criteria: No bearing or winding failures during accelerated life testing equivalent to 20,000 hours.

minds.title: Control System Safety
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: The electronic control system shall maintain safe operation under all single-fault conditions and fail to a safe state.

type_specific_attributes.content: Control system shall detect faults within 50ms and transition to safe state within 200ms of fault detection.
type_specific_attributes.acceptance_criteria: All single-point faults detected and mitigated during FMEA review and hardware-in-the-loop testing.

minds.title: Bearing Wear Exceeds Tolerance
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Progressive bearing wear leads to increased radial play, vibration, and eventual seizure of the motor shaft.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Use sealed deep-groove ball bearings with L10 life rating exceeding 30,000 hours. Implement vibration monitoring.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: Radial play shall not exceed 0.05mm after 20,000 hours

minds.title: Winding Insulation Breakdown
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Degradation of stator winding insulation due to thermal stress, leading to inter-turn short circuits.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Use Class H insulation rated to 180C. Implement thermal protection via PTC thermistors embedded in windings.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: Insulation resistance shall remain above 100 MOhm after thermal cycling tests

minds.title: Sensor Signal Loss
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Loss of feedback signals from position or current sensors causing the control loop to operate without valid input data.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Implement redundant sensor channels with cross-validation. Add signal plausibility checks in firmware.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: System shall detect sensor loss within 10ms and enter safe state within 50ms

minds.title: Firmware Fault Causing Uncontrolled Output
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Software defect or runtime error in the motor controller firmware leading to incorrect PWM output or loss of control.

type_specific_attributes.mitigation_plan: Apply MISRA-C coding standards. Implement independent hardware watchdog and safe-torque-off circuit.
type_specific_attributes.acceptable_limit: Zero uncontrolled output events during 10,000 hours of field operation

minds.title: Excessive Vibration
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Motor exhibits vibration levels exceeding acceptable thresholds during operation, affecting both bearing life and winding integrity.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Vibration amplitude exceeds 4.5 mm/s RMS at rated speed
type_specific_attributes.effects: Accelerated bearing degradation, winding insulation fretting, structural fatigue in mounting brackets
type_specific_attributes.causes: Rotor imbalance, bearing defects, shaft misalignment, loose mounting
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Accelerometer-based vibration monitoring with FFT analysis

minds.title: Shaft Misalignment
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Angular or parallel misalignment between motor shaft and driven load shaft.

type_specific_attributes.effects: Uneven bearing load distribution, coupling wear, increased vibration harmonics at 2x running speed
type_specific_attributes.causes: Manufacturing tolerance stack-up, thermal expansion differential, improper assembly
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Laser alignment measurement during assembly and periodic maintenance

minds.title: Lubrication Failure
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Bearing grease degradation or depletion leading to metal-to-metal contact.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Bearing grease loses viscosity below minimum threshold
type_specific_attributes.effects: Increased friction, elevated bearing temperature, accelerated wear, eventual bearing seizure
type_specific_attributes.causes: Grease oxidation at elevated temperatures, contamination ingress, insufficient initial fill
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Temperature monitoring of bearing housing with trend analysis

minds.title: Thermal Runaway in Stator Windings
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Localized hot spot in stator winding causes progressive insulation degradation and eventual short circuit.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Winding temperature exceeds insulation class rating for sustained period
type_specific_attributes.effects: Inter-turn short circuit, reduced motor efficiency, smoke generation, potential fire hazard
type_specific_attributes.causes: Blocked ventilation, overload condition, ambient temperature exceedance, insulation defect
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: PTC thermistor embedded in end-windings with over-temperature trip

minds.title: EMC Interference on Sensor Lines
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Electromagnetic interference corrupts sensor feedback signals, causing erroneous readings.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Sensor output deviates more than 5% from true value due to conducted or radiated EMI
type_specific_attributes.effects: Control loop instability, torque ripple, potential overcurrent condition
type_specific_attributes.causes: Insufficient shielding on sensor cables, poor PCB layout, switching noise from power stage
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Signal plausibility check comparing redundant sensor channels

minds.title: Memory Corruption in Controller
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: RAM or flash memory corruption in the motor controller MCU causing unpredictable firmware behavior.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Critical control variables corrupted in RAM during operation
type_specific_attributes.effects: Incorrect PWM duty cycle output, loss of current limiting, potential uncontrolled motor acceleration
type_specific_attributes.causes: Single-event upset from EMI, power supply glitch, stack overflow, firmware bug
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: CRC checks on critical data, ECC memory, periodic RAM self-test

minds.title: Watchdog Timer Failure to Reset
minds.creator: fmea-example@example.com
minds.description: Independent watchdog timer fails to trigger system reset when firmware becomes unresponsive.

type_specific_attributes.failure_mode: Watchdog does not reset MCU within specified timeout window
type_specific_attributes.effects: Firmware remains in faulted state, safe-torque-off not activated, continued uncontrolled output
type_specific_attributes.causes: Watchdog clock source failure, incorrect watchdog configuration, common-cause failure with main MCU
type_specific_attributes.detection_method: Independent external watchdog IC with separate clock source and power-on self-test
